facts.claim: Aarif Sheikh was born on October 5, 1997 in Birgunj, Nepal
Right-hand batsman and right-arm medium-pace bowler; all-rounder role
His younger brother Aasif Sheikh also plays for Nepal as wicketkeeper-batsman; Aarif mentored Aasif in their childhood
ODI debut: August 1, 2018 vs Netherlands — Nepal's inaugural official ODI match; Aarif was one of 11 players who made history that day
ODI career: 68 matches, 1,445 runs, average 27.26, highest score 84, strike rate 65.11, 9 wickets, economy 4.7
T20I career: 50 matches, 592 runs, average 18.5, highest score 39, strike rate 111.27, 4 wickets, economy 8.25
Was part of Nepal CWC League 2 2023 campaign where Nepal won 11 of 12 matches
Was NOT selected for Nepal's T20 World Cup 2024 squad; his younger brother Aasif Sheikh WAS selected
Won Player of Tournament in a Kathmandu one-day competition: 261 runs at 52.20 average, 11 wickets
Birgunj is a border city with India; the Nepal-India Raxaul/Birgunj border checkpoint is one of the busiest in South Asia
Aarif and Aasif have played together in Nepal's ODI squad since 2021
